title: Aerospace Engineer
desc: You design planes, rockets, and satellites — figuring out how to make machines fly fast, high, and safely through the sky or even into space.

title: Mechanical Engineer
desc: You design and build the physical parts of machines — engines, gears, pumps — making sure they work efficiently and don't break down.

title: Nuclear Engineer
desc: You work with nuclear reactions to generate energy or design medical equipment, following very strict safety rules because the stakes are extremely high.

title: Automotive Engineer
desc: You design and improve cars — from the engine and brakes to the software that makes them run — making vehicles safer, faster, and more efficient.

title: Materials Scientist
desc: You study and invent new materials — like stronger metals, lighter plastics, or heat-resistant ceramics — that other engineers use to build better products.

title: Petroleum Engineer
desc: You design the equipment and methods used to pull oil and gas out of the ground, often working on rigs in remote locations for weeks at a time.

title: Military / Defense Engineer
desc: You design weapons, vehicles, and systems for the military — like armor, radar, or drones — knowing your work will be used in real-world conflicts.

title: Civil / Structural Engineer
desc: You design bridges, roads, dams, and buildings — making sure the structures people rely on every day are safe and built to last.

title: Biomedical Engineer
desc: You design medical devices — like prosthetic limbs, MRI machines, or pacemakers — that help doctors treat patients and save lives.

title: Environmental Engineer
desc: You design systems that clean up pollution, treat drinking water, and protect ecosystems — making sure human activity doesn't permanently damage the planet.

title: Chemical Engineer
desc: You design the large industrial processes that turn raw chemicals into useful products — like fuel, medicine, or food — on a massive scale.

title: Toy / Consumer Product Designer
desc: You invent and design the toys, gadgets, and everyday products that end up on store shelves — blending creativity with engineering to make things people love.

title: Industrial Designer
desc: You design how manufactured products look and feel — chairs, phones, appliances — making sure they're both beautiful and actually comfortable to use.

title: Robotics Engineer
desc: You build robots — programming their movements, designing their bodies, and connecting their sensors — for use in factories, hospitals, or even on Mars.

title: Manufacturing Engineer
desc: You design the factory floor — figuring out the fastest, safest, and cheapest way to build products at large scale using machines and assembly lines.

title: Architect
desc: You design buildings — homes, schools, skyscrapers — deciding how they look, how space is used inside, and making sure they're safe and beautiful.

title: Urban Planner
desc: You design entire cities and neighborhoods — deciding where parks, roads, and buildings should go to make communities livable, fair, and efficient.

title: Drone / UAV Engineer
desc: You design and build unmanned flying vehicles — from delivery drones to military surveillance aircraft — combining aeronautics, electronics, and software.

title: Field Service Engineer
desc: You travel to customer locations to install, maintain, and repair complex equipment — like industrial machines or medical devices — wherever they're needed.

title: Acoustic Engineer
desc: You control how sound behaves — designing concert halls so music sounds perfect, or making submarines quieter so they can't be detected by sonar.

title: Welder
desc: You use extreme heat to fuse metal pieces together, building everything from skyscraper frames to ship hulls — it's skilled physical work that pays well.

title: Carpenter / Woodworker
desc: You cut, shape, and build things from wood — from house frames and cabinets to custom furniture — using both hand tools and power tools.

title: Stone Mason / Bricklayer
desc: You lay bricks, stone, and concrete to build walls, floors, and structures — it's physically intense outdoor work that creates things built to last centuries.

title: HVAC Technician
desc: You install and repair heating, ventilation, and air conditioning systems — keeping homes, hospitals, and offices at a comfortable temperature year-round.

title: Electrician
desc: You install and repair the electrical wiring in buildings — making sure power flows safely to every outlet, light, and appliance without starting a fire.

title: Plumber
desc: You install and fix the pipes that carry water and gas through buildings — from fixing a leaky faucet to installing an entire plumbing system in a new home.

title: Ironworker / Structural Steel
desc: You connect the giant steel beams that form the skeleton of skyscrapers and bridges — often working hundreds of feet in the air on narrow beams.

title: Heavy Equipment Operator
desc: You operate giant machines — bulldozers, cranes, and excavators — to move earth, lift steel, and build the physical foundation of roads and buildings.

title: Pipefitter
desc: You install and maintain the complex pipe systems in industrial plants and refineries — this is high-skill, high-pay work often done in remote or rugged locations.

title: Construction Manager
desc: You run a construction site — coordinating workers, managing schedules and budgets, and solving problems to make sure a building gets done right and on time.

title: Machinist / CNC Operator
desc: You use precision cutting machines to shape metal parts to exact measurements — making components for engines, aircraft, or surgical tools with zero room for error.

title: Auto Mechanic
desc: You diagnose and repair car problems — from engine failures to brake jobs — using a combination of technical knowledge, specialty tools, and hands-on skill.

title: Boilermaker
desc: You build and repair the massive tanks, boilers, and pressure vessels used in power plants and factories — it's tough, well-paid work in industrial environments.

title: Wind Turbine Technician
desc: You climb inside and on top of 300-foot wind turbines to maintain and repair them — physically demanding work with a real positive impact on clean energy.

title: Solar Panel Installer
desc: You mount and wire solar panels on rooftops and in fields, helping homes and businesses generate their own clean electricity from sunlight.

title: Marine Mechanic / Technician
desc: You repair and maintain boat engines and marine systems — working in boatyards or aboard vessels, keeping everything running on the water.

title: Software Engineer
desc: You write the code that runs apps, websites, and operating systems — spending most of your day at a computer thinking through complex logic problems.

title: Web Developer
desc: You build websites and web apps — coding the buttons, layouts, and features that users interact with whenever they open a browser.

title: Embedded Systems Programmer
desc: You write the tiny, efficient programs that run inside physical devices — like the code controlling a car's brakes or a pacemaker's heartbeat — where bugs can be dangerous.

title: Low-Level / Systems Programmer
desc: You write software extremely close to the hardware — like operating systems or compilers — working in highly technical code that most programmers never touch.

title: Game Developer
desc: You build video games — coding the physics, AI, and graphics engines that make virtual worlds feel alive and fun to play in.

title: Cybersecurity Engineer
desc: You protect computer systems from hackers — finding and patching vulnerabilities before criminals can exploit them to steal data or shut down critical services.

title: Data Scientist
desc: You analyze massive datasets to find hidden patterns — helping companies understand their customers, predict the future, or make better decisions.

title: AI / Machine Learning Engineer
desc: You build the systems that allow computers to learn from data and make decisions on their own — the technology behind self-driving cars, voice assistants, and medical diagnosis.

title: DevOps / Cloud Engineer
desc: You build the automated pipelines and cloud infrastructure that let software teams deploy new code reliably and keep apps running 24/7 without crashing.

title: Mobile App Developer
desc: You build apps for smartphones and tablets — creating the software people use on their phones every day, from social media to fitness trackers.

title: Circuit Design Engineer
desc: You design the electronic circuits inside devices — planning how electricity flows through a chip or board to make a product do exactly what it's supposed to.

title: PCB Designer
desc: You lay out the printed circuit boards inside every electronic device — planning exactly where each component goes so the board fits, works, and can be manufactured.

title: Electrical Product Designer
desc: You design the whole electrical system inside a new product — from the battery to the user interface — making sure everything works together elegantly.

title: Controls / Automation Engineer
desc: You program the systems that make machines run automatically — like the robots in a car factory or the autopilot on an airplane.

title: Power Systems Engineer
desc: You design and manage electrical grids — the massive systems that generate and distribute electricity to cities, making sure the power never goes out.

title: RF / Communications Engineer
desc: You design the wireless communication systems that transmit signals — working on everything from cell towers and satellites to military radar.

title: Instrumentation Technician
desc: You install and calibrate the sensors and measuring devices in factories, power plants, and oil refineries that keep industrial processes safe and precise.

title: Mechatronics Engineer
desc: You combine mechanical, electrical, and software engineering to build smart machines — like robotic arms, automated vehicles, or intelligent manufacturing equipment.

title: IoT / Connected Devices Developer
desc: You build the software and hardware for smart devices — like connected thermostats, smartwatches, or industrial sensors — that talk to each other over the internet.

title: Forensic Engineer
desc: You're a detective for broken things — investigating why bridges collapsed, planes crashed, or products failed in order to prevent the same disaster from happening again.

title: Cryogenics Technician
desc: You work with materials cooled to nearly absolute zero (-270°C) — maintaining superconducting magnets for MRI machines, particle accelerators, and rocket fuel systems.

title: Explosives Engineer / Blaster
desc: You plan and execute controlled explosions — demolishing buildings, clearing rock in mines, or opening tunnels — using precise math and licensed expertise to make big things go boom safely.

title: Animatronics Engineer
desc: You build the mechanical creatures for movies and theme parks — combining robotics, electronics, and sculpture to make dragons, dinosaurs, and alien creatures move and breathe.

title: Underwater Welder / Commercial Diver
desc: You weld and repair metal structures underwater — like oil pipelines and ship hulls — working in one of the most physically demanding and well-paid trades in existence.

title: Subsea Cable Engineer
desc: You design and repair the massive fiber-optic cables that run along the ocean floor, carrying most of the world's internet traffic between continents — working on specialized ships in the open ocean.

title: NDT Technician (Nondestructive Testing)
desc: You're a doctor for infrastructure — using X-rays, ultrasound, and thermal cameras to find cracks and defects inside bridges, aircraft, and nuclear reactors without ever cutting into them.

title: Human Factors Engineer
desc: You study how humans interact with tools and machines, then redesign cockpits, surgical equipment, and interfaces to reduce mistakes and make things feel natural to use.

title: Packaging Engineer
desc: You engineer the containers and boxes that protect products during shipping — making sure a delicate surgical laser arrives intact after traveling across the world, using sustainable materials.

title: Building Automation Systems Engineer
desc: You program the 'brain' of large buildings — making the HVAC, lighting, and security systems in skyscrapers and hospitals all talk to each other automatically to save energy.

title: Elevator & Escalator Mechanic
desc: You install and maintain the complex high-voltage mechanical and software systems inside elevators — one of the most technical and highest-paid hidden trades, with job security as long as buildings exist.

title: Geotechnical Engineer
desc: You study soil and rock behavior to make sure massive structures don't sink or collapse — if a civil engineer designs the bridge, you design the relationship between the bridge and the ground.

title: Avionics Technician
desc: You install and repair the navigation, radar, and communication electronics inside aircraft — in 2025, this includes the high-tech systems for autonomous drones and electric air taxis.

title: Nuclear Reactor Operator
desc: You monitor and control the nuclear fission process inside a power plant — a trade-school career that requires years of simulation training and earns a six-figure salary without a four-year degree.

title: Flavor & Fragrance Engineer
desc: You design the industrial systems that create consistent tastes and smells at massive scale — understanding the chemistry of scents and the engineering of giant chemical reactors to make food and perfume.

title: Automotive Security Engineer
desc: You hack cars for a living — legally testing whether someone could remotely disable a vehicle's brakes, unlock its doors, or spoof its GPS through the software running inside modern vehicles.

title: ICS/SCADA Security Engineer
desc: You protect the computer systems that control power grids, water plants, and gas pipelines — if a hacker attacked these systems, cities could go dark or water could be poisoned.

title: Physical Penetration Tester
desc: Companies hire you to break into their own buildings — using lock picks, disguises, and social engineering to test whether their physical security can actually keep bad guys out.

title: Medical Device Security Specialist
desc: You hack pacemakers and insulin pumps — legally — to find security flaws before criminals do, protecting patients from attackers who could interfere with life-saving equipment wirelessly.

title: Firmware Reverse Engineer
desc: You take apart the permanent code burned into hardware — routers, smart fridges, military equipment — to find hidden backdoors and vulnerabilities that normal software security would never find.

title: Satellite & Aerospace Security Engineer
desc: You simulate attacks on satellites and the ground stations that control them — finding ways an attacker might hijack a spacecraft's orbit or intercept its high-frequency data transmissions.

title: Red Team Social Engineer
desc: You use psychology instead of code — calling employees and tricking them into revealing passwords, or leaving fake infected USB drives in parking lots, to test whether a company's people are its weakest security link.

title: Hardware Trojan Hunter
desc: You use electron microscopes and X-rays to inspect microchips at the microscopic level, searching for tiny malicious circuits secretly inserted during manufacturing that could compromise military or government hardware.

title: Bug Bounty Hunter
desc: You legally hack companies like Apple, Google, and Tesla in your own time — if you find a real vulnerability before criminals do, the company pays you a 'bounty' that can range from $500 to $100,000.

title: Drone Swarm Coordinator
desc: You control hundreds of drones simultaneously using AI software — assigning group goals while the drones communicate with each other to avoid collisions and complete missions autonomously.

title: Counter-UAS Systems Engineer
desc: You design systems that detect, track, and disable enemy drones — combining radar, thermal imaging, and electronic warfare jamming technology to protect airspace from drone threats.

title: Loitering Munitions Technician
desc: You maintain 'kamikaze drones' — one-way weapons that fly to a target and detonate — calibrating the explosive payloads and optical systems that allow the drone to autonomously recognize its target.

title: Directed Energy (Laser) Weapons Specialist
desc: You work on high-energy laser systems that shoot down missiles and drones at the speed of light — managing the extreme physics, thermal systems, and precision optics of actual working laser weapons.

title: BVLOS Operations Supervisor
desc: You manage drone missions that happen hundreds of miles away from the operator — overseeing the satellite links, data relays, and regulatory compliance required to keep a drone connected over the horizon.

title: Electronic Warfare Scenario Writer
desc: You design realistic simulations of enemy GPS jamming and radio spoofing attacks — writing the 'playbooks' that train AI pilots to recognize when their signals are being manipulated.

title: Bio-Inspired Robotics Engineer
desc: You build drones and robots that mimic nature — flapping-wing drones that fly like birds, or spider-like robots that crawl through rubble — using biomimicry to create machines that avoid detection.

title: Exoskeleton Integration Technician
desc: You maintain the wearable robotic suits that help soldiers carry heavier loads — calibrating the hydraulics, sensors, and battery systems so the machine moves perfectly in sync with the human body.

title: UAS Data Scientist (Sensor Fusion)
desc: You build the AI brain that merges LiDAR, thermal, and camera data from drones into a single 3D map — giving commanders the ability to see through walls or smoke in real-time.

title: Autonomous Surface Vessel (ASV) Pilot
desc: You operate unmanned sea drones that patrol for underwater mines and track enemy ships — combining traditional maritime navigation skills with cutting-edge autonomous robotics.

title: ZK-Proof Engineer (Zero-Knowledge Cryptographer)
desc: You use advanced mathematics to prove things are true without revealing any actual data — making blockchains private, fast, and secure in ways that were impossible just a few years ago.

title: Agentic AI Systems Engineer
desc: You build AI systems that can plan and execute complex multi-step tasks on their own — like an AI that books a flight, makes a payment, and handles a refund without any human input.

title: Real World Asset Tokenization Architect
desc: You design systems that turn physical assets — like real estate or fine art — into digital tokens that can be traded instantly on a global blockchain, connecting Wall Street to crypto.

title: AI Ethics & Compliance Officer
desc: You make sure AI systems aren't biased, don't break privacy laws, and meet government safety standards — you're a translator between legal requirements and the engineers who build the models.

title: Tokenomics Architect
desc: You design the economic rules that govern how a cryptocurrency flows through an ecosystem — like a digital economist ensuring the token's value doesn't collapse and users are rewarded fairly.

title: AI Infrastructure Rack Engineer
desc: You design the massive physical data centers and specialized server racks that house thousands of GPUs needed to train AI models — AI isn't just code, it's enormous hardware.

title: Smart Contract Security Auditor
desc: You're a specialized hacker for crypto — finding vulnerabilities in blockchain code before it goes live, because a single bug can lead to a $100 million theft.

title: AI Prompt Architect (Agent Orchestration)
desc: You design the complex multi-step instruction chains that make different AI models work together to solve enterprise problems — far beyond just chatting with an AI.

title: DePIN Ecosystem Coordinator
desc: You manage decentralized networks where regular people are paid in crypto to provide real-world services — like 5G hotspots, weather sensors, or mapping data — building infrastructure without a central company.

title: AI-Driven On-Chain Data Scientist
desc: You use AI to scan billions of public blockchain transactions in real-time — detecting money laundering, predicting market moves, or tracking 'whale' investors before anyone else sees the pattern.
